mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate sitting near the zero lower bound (0.18%) signals maximal monetary accommodation, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and supporting risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward return during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 8%, indicating severe labor market slack consistent with recession conditions, equity markets price in weaker corporate earnings and depressed consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return conditional on UNRATE > 8% is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across the sample, or hit rate of negative returns falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of near-zero FEDFUNDS and elevated UNRATE (>7%) signals aggressive countercyclical policy accommodation deployed against a severe labor shock; markets anticipate policy-driven recovery and re-rate equities upward over the following month, an effect not captured by either series alone.
failure_condition: Conditional mean 20-day return under joint trigger is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is negative versus single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, a rebound in housing starts (HOUST) signals renewed consumer confidence and durable-goods demand, which forward-predicts broader economic recovery and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 583.0 thousand units in snapshot
regime_hint: post-shock recovery from pandemic-driven demand collapse
failure_condition: In periods following a HOUST uptrend, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects an improving supply-side capacity utilization trend that leads short-horizon equity momentum as markets react to incoming production data, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 500,000 jobs signals an acute labor market shock that triggers immediate risk-off equity selling, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133019.0 thousand persons in snapshot
failure_condition: 5-day forward return following a PAYEMS decline > 500k is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or hit rate of negative returns falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month surge in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 5% signals stronger-than-expected consumer demand recovery, prompting upward revisions to earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344380.0 million dollars in snapshot
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return following a >5% RSAFS surge is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL growth stalls or declines while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, markets price in a liquidity-trap deflationary risk regime where monetary policy loses traction, causing risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, an effect that emerges only from the joint low-inflation/low-rate configuration.
regime_hint: zero lower bound deflationary liquidity trap
failure_condition: Conditional mean 60-day return under joint trigger is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the interaction's marginal Rank IC contribution versus single-series baselines is negative or negligible.
